original_hypothesis.title: TimelockTokenPool.void() Interaction Allows Owner to Manipulate Vesting Post-Void
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When void() is called, _voidGrant sets _grant.amount = amountOwned (what's already vested), then zeros grantStart and grantPeriod. After voiding, _getAmountOwned returns _grant.amount directly (since _start == 0 → return _amount). However, the unlock schedule (unlockStart/unlockCliff/unlockPeriod) remains unchanged. The voided recipient's tokens become fully 'owned' but still subject to the original unlock schedule. If the owner calls void() strategically (right before a vesting cliff), the recipient loses tokens they were about to vest. The owner could re-grant these voided tokens to a colluding party.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The void mechanism allows the owner to claw back unvested tokens at any point. Combined with the ability to re-grant voided tokens (totalAmountVoided increases, freeing capacity for new grants), this creates an asymmetric power dynamic where the owner can strategically time voids to maximize clawback.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _voidGrant computes amountVoided = _grant.amount - amountOwned; clawback is immediate and irreversible for recipient

preconditions: Owner has motive to claw back tokens
Owner times void() call right before major vesting milestone

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: amountVoided = _grant.amount - _getAmountOwned(grant); timing determines vested vs voided split
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner calls void(recipient) at timestamp just before cliff, maximizing clawback
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Owner can claw back up to (amount - currentlyVested) tokens and redistribute

economic_questions: What is the total value of unvested grants across all recipients?
Is the owner address protected by timelock/multisig?
Can voided amount be immediately re-granted to a different address?
validation_requirements: Calculate maximum clawback per recipient at various timestamps
Verify void + re-grant flow: void(A) then grant(B, voidedAmount)
Check if governance protections prevent malicious void timing

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to total unvested tokens across all recipients

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Just before major vesting cliff dates

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Map all recipient grant schedules and cliff dates
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Compute maximum owner-extractable value at each time point

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
